posts.title: Why I love shore crabs
posts.excerpt: The humble green shore crab is one of the most successful animals on the Cornish coast — here's why it fascinates me.
posts.body:
The green shore crab (*Carcinus maenas*) is everywhere on the Cornish coast — and that's exactly what makes it so interesting.

## A survivor

They tolerate huge swings in temperature, salinity and oxygen. That resilience is part of why they've become one of the most widespread invasive species in the world.

## What I'm studying

This summer I've been running a mark-recapture study to estimate their population in a local estuary. More on that soon.

posts.title: A morning on the rocky shore
posts.excerpt: Notes from a dawn survey at Gyllyngvase — what the low tide revealed.
posts.body:
There's nothing quite like a spring low tide at dawn.

We recorded 17 species across three transects. Here's what stood out and what it tells us about the health of this shore.
